metadata.notes: Participation in IETF meetings constitutes agreement to the Note Well, which permits recording, transcription, and public archival of proceedings. See also BCP 78 (RFC 5378) and BCP 79 (RFC 8179) for IPR policies.

transcript.text: you you good morning good morning welcome everyone so half hour later then plans but i think it was good to wait for everyone to join tomorrow for the next 15 minutes i want to introduce you a little bit in the echoton how things are going how to find the project of your interest and start thinking about your presentation for tomorrow and of course times for lunch and dinner good the hackathon and we always start with this kind of raising hand so for whom is this the first good  see it's always a great way to start a weekend or the week actually during this weekend to discuss ideas drafts and next question for whom is this the first idea event okay over there handfold to handfold to get to know people for the rest of the week if you want to join a project of your interest in any topics please reach out to me my name is Beno and very are you  not in the room at the right moment very is my colleague here also coach here and he can also help you in finding the proper project to work with or any other introduction future why are we here so one of the important things of the hackathon is to  standards are relevant if they are used and if they are used they have to be an implementation so deploy it standards are relevant and you can just deploy and well you need implementation so one of the important things here during the weekend is to have this collaborative spirit of the open source community in the room discuss ideas implement ideas  compatibility of interoperability tests to make progress on your ITF drafts another thing of course is to attract new people into the ITF and also developers and young professionals and and university engagement and that was pretty reasonable well pretty successful through the year so that's that's one of the important and I think also one of the successes of the ITF hackathon and one of the well kind of running  themes here at the ITF is there we believe in rough consensus that's in the working groups and running code and that's what we're doing here today so that's why also the hackathon is that important for the ITF good ITF the hackathon this is an ITF session so for ITF sessions some rules apply that's the note well so I won't go into the note well but be sure to read the note well as you start at the ITF also this weekend it's about how IPR is managed how people engage with these other rules of engagement good so now it will apply also here and for your code it's a little bit different the code is yours the code there's not an ITF contribution but the discussions around that  contributions all your presentation our ITF contribution so it will end up on the website of the ITF as a presentation if there are anything that needs clarification ask me or Barry and we can explain how the rules are how things are but yeah the code is not ITF contribution but everything you discuss are ITF contributions oh and a usual ITF copyright and IPR disclosures applies here it's good to know good projects and a lot of projects on the vkey so thank you for everyone that filled in the projects that Lisa, the projects in the vkey you're very useful and as a good archive of all the things all the activities during this weekend thank you if you're not sure yet which projects you want to contribute look at the lost and found people can present their skills and otherwise people that have a project that looking for skills use that or ask me or Barry again or send an email to the Hackathon shares you want to help here  the agenda for today well we're now around 11 during the Hackathon kickoff so you're formed for teams just after this one around 1230 will be lunch and about 630 will be dinner served and around 9 before 9 we will close the room also because the staff wants to go home so I want most of the times I need to clear the room it's yeah it's fun to stay for a little bit later and have other discussions just work in Hackathon good the projects on the Hackathon vkey oh sorry this is next slide tomorrow again we started 930 oh this one mistake here it's again the lunches at 1230 I will change that and upload the news slides at 1230 but important at 1330 you stop with programming and you start preparing a short presentation about three slides four slides maybe but you only have three minutes so it will be very precise what you want to communicate until during two o'clock and four o'clock we ask everyone who wants to submit the presentation three minutes to present your results what's the problem you want to all right i will come to that later so good there will be quite some remote participants the remote participants  the URL is also on the wiki i will skip this the people that use that already they are aware how that works the Hackathon space of your colleagues will be a remote and you will be here in the room you can work also with the get-at-time the works pretty well and some teams are completely remote and they work on get-at-time if anyone needs special network requests it's mostly already announced so if you have special network requests look at HackNet or ask again me or Barry for support this is important and we will repeat this part also tomorrow the presentation of results for that two tomorrow so you only have three minutes so be very think very carefully about what you want to present so what's the problem you want to solve what do you have achieved in the past two days and something like who goes if lessons learned feedback through the working group etc and your team of course but don't go into details you don't have time to present go about the idea and what you have achieved okay you can use data tracker to submit your slides it's like a working group so you need of course your data tracker login and then you can submit your slides how to do that you go the policies for here for the screenshot of 244 so let's it's similar so you go to the Sunday agenda at the two o'clock slot from the Hackathon and you find the icon over there upload slides in data tracker and there you follow the menu and you go to the slides tab upload slides you can upload your slides PDF into the data tracker and there will be ready to be presented in the METECO  again I will present it tomorrow again before to a clock because it takes a lot of organization and an encode ination from our side to have this late submission in place remember it must be in PDF format and don't change names of your presentation if you have a revision if you use the same name the revision will be replaced in the original otherwise we have to have all kind of duplicates hanging around it's it and there's also an ITF Hackathon hit repo I will update it soon and you can find a PowerPoint or an HTML template for your presentations and in the ITF Hackathon repo you can also present or create your own repository in the GitHub ITF Hackathon repository organization for visibility and for if you want to continue work in the next ITF Hackathon good that's it I will skip this one remember what you achieved here you will present on Sunday to the people in the room on Monday you can present your results to the whole ITF so we have to be called at the Hack Hack demo happy hour it's at 6 from 6 to 7 on Monday evening you get a table you can give a short demonstration you can use posters and you can engage with the ITF Hackathon repository and you can see that's not this weekend here in the room it's really fun there will be a paid bar for drinks but there will be snacks and soft drinks available yeah it's on the second level for you six o'clock seven o'clock and please resist your team  good code lounge is on the fourth these level and this is a large room to collaborate  think I haven't been there but I think on the map it looks great and you can collaborate also on your running code  want to thank the sponsors for today this weekend the CNIC and I can sponsor the Hackathon they provide for the drinks in the food so I want to thank our sponsors thank you any questions you can find me here at the front of the table and I want also to invite and out of the ordinary project here but they want to have your inputs please go ahead just a really quick note there's a table number 20 at the back corner with Greg who's standing up and waving and Duro will be here later it's a little bit different kind of project it's one looking at get hug documentation so if you're working with get hug to do documents the author of them or to review them or in any way used in an working group we've heard a lot of comments about the lack of good documentation in the IITF and so we're really looking for suggestions on how to improve it so come visit us and we'll only take a few minutes here time thank you very much yeah so please visit the table and have a chat I will definitely do that thank you for your attention and start on your project and probably I will announce in more than an hour lunch so we will see me around success you
